campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary ordinance and moving forward with plans for a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant money to fund the project.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth scholarship fund
City extending temporary ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17 - feasibility study approved
Grant application submitted for bike/pedestrian infrastructure

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met to decide which organizations would receive grant money for events and activities in Saratoga. They approved funding amounts for specific groups, set up a new competitive grant program for events happening this fiscal year, and made updates to the rules for how community events and street closures are approved and funded.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive event grants with set dollar amounts
Launched Competitive Event Grant Program for fiscal year funding
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications
Allocated total funding across multiple recipients

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, online streams, and phone call-in options. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages to make it accessible to the community.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible via Cable Channel 26 and online platforms
Phone dial-in option for residents without internet access

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council cancelled a zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026. The hearing has been rescheduled for April 8, 2026, so if you were planning to attend or had zoning-related business, you'll need to mark the new date on your calendar.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning matters affected

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal community development grants that help fund local housing and assistance programs. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support residents in need.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for housing and community assistance programs
Review of funding requests from local organizations
Approval of previous meeting records

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed in Cupertino. They discussed ways to reach out to shop owners about available support programs, including a business license amnesty program, summer shopping campaigns to encourage residents to buy local, and financial incentives for retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get current on licenses
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to boost local retail sales
Outreach to shopping centers about city support programs
Financial incentives and tax breaks for small businesses and startups
City partnerships to sponsor local events

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they postponed several items for later discussion. They approved a plan to remove overgrown weeds around the city as part of a county-wide program, and they asked residents for feedback on how federal housing money should be spent in Santa Clara over the next year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026
Federal housing grant funding decisions (HUD)
Multiple agenda items postponed to future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine matters. The council recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software (Mimecast) costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency shelter agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report additions to agenda
